meaning: n.元帅,典礼官,执行官,司仪官vt.整顿,配置,汇集vi.排列,集合
examples: Air Chief Marshal Glenn Torpy told the graduates, "This is probably one of the most significant milestones in an aviator's career."
Police were brought in to marshal the crowd.
He acted as grand marshal of a stock car race.
If he wanted to, Romney could probably marshal some solid evidence on his bureaucracy claim.
These conglomerates can easily marshal a global sales force to land and support new customers.
